Layered composite: 20mm bolted applique + 50mm base plate, computed via layered_plate_effective_thickness(first_hit_mm=20, underlying_mm=50)=56.0mm (Ch.9 p.38 formula, validated against the chapter's own 3 worked examples this session). face_hardened_fraction=50/70=0.714 reflects that only the base plate (within the confirmed 30-50mm 1942 policy bracket) is face-hardened, not the bolt-on applique -- blended via ArmorPlate.face_hardened_fraction rather than the all-or-nothing face_hardened flag. This project's own construction (how to blend partial face-hardening), not directly sourced -- see design spec S17/19. heat_thickness_mm/heat_vertical_deg overridden to the raw 70mm/21deg: Ch.9's layered-plate discount is derived from kinetic plugging-shear test data and there is no sourced basis for applying the same discount to a HEAT jet's penetration mechanism -- deliberately NOT applying the kinetic-specific correction to HEAT rather than assuming it carries over.

DECIDED, not face-hardened: Ch.3 p.22 names StuG III among vehicles retaining face-hardened armor late-war, but only names Panther and PzKpfw IVH specifically for 'thicker plates' beyond the base 30-50mm policy bracket -- this 80mm glacis isn't confirmed at that thickness the way PzIV's 85mm driver plate was (a direct worked example). Also considered and rejected: treating this as a layered 50mm-base+30mm-applique composite the way Panzer III's hull front was resolved (design spec S20) -- no source confirms StuG III Ausf G's specific 80mm glacis was built this way (vs. a single thicker rolled/cast plate), and inventing that construction without evidence would be a guess, not a resolution. Final call: leave unflagged until a source specifically addresses StuG III's own glacis construction.

Isigny-measured actual glacis (85mm@57deg), not nominal 80mm@55deg, per Ch.6's own recommendation. Slope-only cross-validated against source's own parallel 76mm-vs-glacis calc (236-248mm). Medium flaw multiplier applied (Ch.6) -- cross-validated against two of the chapter's own Panther-glacis worked examples (152mm APBC and 17pdr APCBC, both within ~1%). 'Medium' chosen as representative since source suggests ~50% of production had some flaw severity, ranging small-large; not a claim every glacis is exactly medium-flawed.
